We reached out to Deeley King Pang & Van Etten for assistance with a simple dispute over a severance agreement. Given their reputation and the relatively straightforward nature of the case, we trusted them to handle the matter efficiently and effectively. Unfortunately, over a year later, we are left with an unresolved dispute and the added burden of paying for their services.

The initial consultation went smoothly, and Steve King reassured us that our case would be managed promptly. However, as time went on, we encountered a series of issues:

Poor Communication: We were disappointed by the lack of initiative from our attorney. Draft letters that were prepared for us were lacking in substance and clarity. At one point, we were informed that a settlement offer had been made, only to later discover that the terms did not align with our agreed goals.

Unjustified Costs: Despite the simplicity of the matter, we were billed for many hours of work that seemed unnecessary and unproductive. The breakdown of charges included reading our emails and repeatedly rewriting drafts. Ultimately, after terminating our agreement, we received an additional $200 bill simply to answer a question about how to pay the previous invoice.

No Resolution: A year has passed, and the issue remains only partially resolved. This ongoing delay has caused undue stress and financial strain.

It is incredibly frustrating to feel misled by a firm that labeled this as a ‘simple case.’ We initially believed Deeley King Pang & Van Etten would provide competent and efficient service, and we were willing to pay for that assurance. However, after over a year and several thousand dollars later, I regretfully cannot recommend their services. If you value clear communication, transparency, and effective legal support, I highly advise looking elsewhere.
Never again

Hello, I was in a car accident three years ago. Initially, I was referring the case to a large law firm and receiving treatment when I received a call from the law firm. They told me I couldn't handle this case, that it was too difficult, and that I should find another lawyer. Suddenly, they suggested that since I didn't have a lawyer, they should handle it with my insurance company and the other party's company. They said they would cover car repairs, hospital bills, and no insurance premium increases. I was heartbroken. Why is this case so difficult? The at-fault driver should be punished, but let's just settle as if nothing happened. I felt so wronged. I decided to find another lawyer. If I was at fault, they should be punished. Where things went south is when they were asking for a 350 consultation fee before even seeing if you have a case. That was a huge red flag from the moment I reached out. So you’re telling me that I have to pay 350 bucks with out even knowing if I have a case?.What if you don’t have a case? You still have to pay that 350 consultation fee?  In my eyes that’s robbery at its finest. Save your self the trouble and look elsewhere. No law firm is above nor better than a person simply looking for help.
reviews.ownerResponse: Aloha. I believe you are mistaken. Our office policy is that we have the prospective clients fill out the form and we review it to see if they should have a consult. If we think they should have a consult based on what they submit, we set up the consult and charge for the consult. No one here thinks they are better than anyone else. We are in the business of helping people who have been wronged. Sorry if you got any other impression.

reviews.text:
A Law Office that will genuinely have your back!
No one wants to require legal representation but when bad times fall on decent people, they deserve to have genuine experts to help them in their time of need. This Law Office is exactly that. My case was handled with great care right from the beginning. Joseph Rosenbaum was very informative and he and his legal team were always available to answer any of my questions. The generosity and knowledge from this law office put my mind at ease. Always quick to respond and always made sure that I fully understood what was going on. I am not only pleased with the outcome but also with how easy the experience was for me. I highly recommend Fujiwara & Rosenbaum to anyone who is looking for empathetic and professional representation.

I contacted this law office in search for a specific civil attorney who would be able to take my case. The front desk individual was completely incompetent and I had to repeat myself at least 5 times to him to give my phone number and email, monotoned and unprofessional. Definitely not elevated and a poor representation for a professional law firm
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Katayama. While we cannot go into the detail, an employee of ours who is no longer with us was having some medical issues. We appreciate your understanding

reviews.text: I met Joseph thanks to an unfortunate need with an employer issue.  He was by far one of the best lawyers I have ever had to deal with on either side of the employment spectrum.  I highly recommend him and his team for any legal advice you may need.
I had Mr. Rosenbaum represent me and we went to trial. We won!!!

Thank goodness you don't always need a lawyer, but when you do, you REALLY need one and it definitely helps to have someone competent, knowledgeable and down to earth that you can rely on. Mr. Rosenbaum immediately puts you at ease with his caring manner and expertise in legalese-and if he doesn't know precisely, he'll find out. Thanks Mr. Rosenbaum, our family is grateful to have you as our attorney.

I had Mr. Rosenbaum represent me in court. We went to trial and Mr.Rosenbaum didnt even have to put me on the stand. Mr. Rosenbaum pointed out to the court that the prosecutor did not get sufficient facts on the record and did not meet its burden. As such the judge dismissed my case even before it was our turn to put on our case. I would recommend Mr. Rosenbaum for any person needing a trial attorney. Mr.Rosenbaum is a great civil right attorney!

I reached out to Fujiwara and Rosenbaum when my employer promised me yet refused to pay for my health insurance when it was required under Hawai'i law. The employer knew that I was taking the job for insurance specifically. I complained and was wrongfully terminated. Joseph Rosenbaum at Fujiwara and Rosenbaum in Honolulu help me rectify this situation. Mr. Rosenbaum went up against one of the top corporate employment attorneys in Honolulu and the case proceeded to resolution with Mr. Rosenbaum winning at the hearing. He provided effective representation and his ethical standard is beyond reproach. I would recommend him to anyone trying to find a competent and passionate civil rights attorney. Dealing with Mr. Rosenbaum is like talking to a friend. He is not pretentious, but is highly intelligent when it comes to court litigation. This guy is on it.

By the way because I was in a tough position at the time, Mr. Rosenbaum didn't charge me a dime.

Fujiwara and Rosenbaum have provided me with legal services on many occasions relating to business and personal. They have fought for my rights and prevailed, always keeping my goals in mind. I would highly recommend them to anybody looking for an honest and competent attorney.
The best lawyer in Oahu, he protects you and knows what's best for you!

I had an exceptional experience speaking with Mr Parsons about my civil case. He spent hours with me, patiently going through the details and truly trying to understand the situation. I have never interacted with a lawyer who was so human, attentive, warm, and genuinely caring.
He didn’t rush the conversation and made me feel heard and supported throughout the entire consultation. His professionalism combined with such kindness is rare to find.
I highly recommend anyone reading this review to reach out to their office. You can feel confident knowing you are in very good hands.
I have known Wayne for years. He is an exceptional lawyer and one of the finest people I know

Was calling around to find information and perhaps an attorney who'd represent my wife, who was hit by a car while crossing the street.
Mr. Parsons gave freely of his time to answer my questions and offer advice regarding how to achieve medical-cost reimbursement in Hawaii as a no-fault state.
I'm extremely grateful for his feedback and willingness to take his time with me. His advice was very helpful.

Wayne is a fabulous lawyer.  He cares deeply about his clients and fights for them to obtain justice.  He is also committed to making the world a better place through his community service.   You can't do better than Wayne if you need a lawyer in Hawaii.
I was desperate for legal help dealing with my insurance company, I was rescued by Mr. Parsons and his Staff.  Unquestionably the best there is...bar none.

“Our practice is infused with our passion for community and the aloha spirit. Our attorneys are welcoming, courteous and thoughtful.”

I was told I would get a call back 4 times and I never did. I would expect with all the claims of kindness and decency on their web page they would at least return a call to help me during this unfortunate time in my families life. I wasted a whole month and a half unanswered. I even picked this law office because they said they can handle military divorce.

LIFE CHANGING & THE HARDEST BATTLES OF OUR LIVES!!!!

No case is the same & everyone's legal journey is different.....

Need that someone to have your back 100 & treat you like 'ohana? Well...TRUST ME when I say look no further. No amount of money could ever show my appreciation to Greg Frey & Shannon Hackett! Their entire team is UNMATCHED! Greg represented me and a few years later Shannon represented my husband and we both were beyond blessed at our final hearings! All the hard work put into both cases was worth every second & penny!

In my case I was served papers and never felt so scared in my entire life that my daughter could be taken from me. I felt helpless but gathered myself together & Greg popped into my head. We met in person I had binders, notebooks, cell phones (all types of evidence)... let me just say we didn't need to open any of it during court cause he's just thaaaat good. He fought for everything and more for me!

It's sad to hear that FATHERS think "oh the state favors ONLY the MOTHERS" oh boy would you have loved to sit in my husband's hearings cause not 1 but 2 different judges RIPPED the other party multiple times (satisfying when the other party got power trips lol). Judges admit they hear sad stories all the time but when you have FIRE LAWYERS defending you and your rights you have that PEACE OF MIND knowing whose got you and know exactly how to do what they do in the "Legal World". I encourage more FATHERS to FIGHT for their keiki and their parental rights! When it's for your keiki you do anything & everything for them!

Mahalo Nunui to Greg, Shannon, & their entire team....we have keiki that are sOo happy we did what we had to do for them. Doing whats best for your keiki is PRIORITY.

One of the worst legal experiences I’ve ever had.
ZERO STAR

I hired Stefanie L. Prinsloo, Esq. at Coates Frey & Hackett, AAL LLLC for my divorce, and it was one of the biggest regrets of this entire process. Instead of helping me, she made everything worse. She didn’t advocate for me, didn’t fight for my rights, and frankly, left me feeling more alone, confused, and unprotected than before I had legal representation.
She is a new and inexperienced lawyer she even had to Google basic military-related legal questions during our meetings. It felt like she was more concerned about staying neutral or pleasing the opposing party than standing up for me. She constantly forgot details I had shared with her, including what I wanted in my divorce decree. I had to catch her mistakes and ask her to fix them.
She gave me zero guidance on how to handle custody or scheduling with my children’s father. For two years, I’ve been trying to get through this divorce, and I still haven’t made it to my first rebuttal hearing. She rescheduled that hearing to pursue mediation but although she technically requested it, she didn’t fight to make it happen. She sent only two emails to the opposing party and then gave up when they didn’t respond no follow-up, no urgency. That delay alone wasted over four months. I was left stuck, thinking I had to give my kids to their father whenever he asked, because I had no legal guidance and didn’t want to seem like I was withholding them.
Because of her lack of proper guidance, I was forced to handle my child’s father on my own. He was stuck at his military duty station and needed my help because our child was enrolled in the EFMP (Exceptional Family Member Program). He needed me to bridge the gap between EFMP in Texas and Hawaii. I had every legal right not to help him especially considering how emotionally abusive and manipulative he had been toward me. But my lawyer advised me to assist him: to help with paperwork, get a physical done, and make sure everything was submitted so he could get transferred to Hawaii.
Big mistake. After he relocated here, he became even more toxic claiming my home was unsafe, saying he should be the primary parent, trying to avoid child support, and refusing to give me access to the money we saved over our 7-year marriage. Helping him only opened a new door of hell. He has now gone as far as making vile accusations including claiming our child was molested just to gain control. He took the kids whenever he wanted for four months straight without a custody schedule, all because I followed my lawyer’s weak and dangerous advice. After hiring a new attorney, I was told I never should’ve helped him with his military transfer, and I should’ve refused to let him take the children without a proper agreement in place.  I spent $2,000 just on rescheduling the rebuttal, and $9,000 total with absolutely nothing to show for it. No progress, no support, and no peace of mind.
Honestly, this law firm doesn’t fight for you they just push paperwork. That’s all they do. They will drag your case out just to nickel and dime you. You’re better off using ChatGPT to understand your legal rights and handle your own filings than wasting your money here. For nearly $10,000, I got stress, delays, and no real representation.
I understand that divorce is expensive and that good legal help comes at a price. What I don’t accept is spending thousands only to be left defending myself, scared, and manipulated while my lawyer sat back and did nothing. She wasted my time, increased my stress, and left me to face everything alone. I would not recommend her to anyone, especially if you are dealing with a complex or emotional case involving custody, abuse, or military matters. You deserve better.

I was a client in 2022, and in 2025, I requested a copy of my custody agreement from your firm, only to be informed that there would be a $250 fee to retrieve my records from storage. Following this, I sought a consultation with a different law firm, which not only retrieved my case from the courts for a significantly cheaper fee but also provided a complimentary consultation. Consequently, I have chosen to retain the services of this new firm.

I would like to express my appreciation for the results I achieved in 2022 with the assistance of my attorney from Coates & Frey, who was truly exceptional; however, he has since retired.

Got what I wanted and a little extra😉 The office gives early 2000’s disorganized musty school office but it’s there. Jeffy jeff was such a treat. I made this to remember him always 🤍🖤🤍 RIP to your pro bono family lawyer, but he’s built different 😘🤤

Super professional, and quickly completed our court case hearing. Yes, they were pricey, but we paid for quality work. I would always recommend this law firm to others. They helped get the adoption of our son done in a timely and efficient manner. They communicated with us constantly, and kept us in the loop. They provided all the essential paper work for us, as well as helped navigate the filling out of certain documents. Very knowledgeable attorneys, and satisfied with the work that was completed.

We worked with Shannon and it’s crazy to think anyone would have negative things to say about her or this law firm. They have been nothing but efficient and professional in helping with my son’s adoption case. Yea we paid a pretty penny but truthfully it was worth every cent. 10/10 would recommend. Mahalo nui for all of your guys help!

Nothing is ever easy in a divorce, but having dependable and reliable legal counsel makes the process much more bearable.  I really appreciated the honesty and professionalism that Shannon Hackett provided.  Her knowledge and expertise were brilliant and she was very thorough.  Her team was quick with follow through and clear in communicating.  I would definitely recommend Coates, Frey and Hackett in the future.

I was referred to Shannon Hackett by another attorney. Through years of searching for an attorney on island who had time for my case, I found Shannon to be the most available and prompt. Highly recommend! Shannon is further proof that women should run the world.
Shannon and her staff were awesome during a difficult time in my life. If I was able to offer more than five stars I certainly would.

I've worked with Shannon Hackett for the last 2 years and what and honor and privilege. She is quick on her feet in court, wise, thorough and makes the time to understand the breadth of the situation so she can represent you well. Her paralegals are great and it's been a pleasure working with her and her team. I appreciated how Shannon is efficient, a hard worker, a good communicator and that she cares. I highly recommend working with her if you have the opportunity to.
Great experience with Shannon. My divorce was done in a quick and efficient manner. Mahalo Nui Loa

I was in a situation I didn't think I would need to deal with.  The knowledge, compassion and patience Shannon Hackett and her team has made it easier to cope with and the case went by quickly.  Hopefully I will not need them in the future, but if I do I will definitely contact them again.

Shannon was AWESOME.  We were needing help with a legal guardianship and she was responsive, compassionate, thorough in her explanations of the steps, and patient with my lack of understanding with the legal system.  Moana, her assistant, was attentive, professional and timely with every request.  We couldn't be happier with the whole experience and Shannon and her team will be the ones we call when we are ready to take the next steps.

God bless you and your associates.

Shannon Hackett handled my adoption case and I couldn’t be happier! Great communication and set realistic expectations for our case every step of the way. Great experience from day 1 and have already recommended this firm to friends and family.

I enjoyed my experience with this firm. They were cooperative and very communicative, facilitating my divorce, and eventual, reconciliation. If the need should arise, I would absolutely go back to this firm. More specifically, hire Mrs. Hackett again.

Attitude problems… wants money but doesn’t want to provide correct and proper representation for you the only thing one star is for their polite assistant / receptionist girls also i don’t how the other lawyers in firm are but mine specifically I regret! She screwed me over when I really needed legal help in my paternity case…

I hired Coates, Frey, Tanimoto, and Gibson for a Child Support proceeding and have regretted the decision ever since. Tom Tanimoto was more concerned with handing the opposing party what they wanted than fighting for a fathers rights.
His lack of communication is painful.
I am now cleaning up the messes he made and doing a better job
In my opinion, this is a less than average law firm.
IF YOU ARE A FATHER, I strongly suggest finding a different firm to defend you.

I found them to be weak at representation and it was also strange that they were not giving me advice on the divorce decree parameters, like they wanted to avoid me later saying that they mislead me. I am an attorney but not a divorce attorney so I really needed advice or at least some discussion. My divorce was a long time ago, so maybe this aspect of their practice has evolved; or maybe not.
Compassionate and professional
Fantastic firm with experienced, professional, and friendly attorneys
I called and the receptionist completely wasted my time.
The attorneys did a great jo in settling my personal injury claim.  Am pleased with the results.  attorney and staff were pleasant and helpful during the entire process.

I could not be more pleased with the team we had the pleasure of working with. From start to finish; the communication was great! Always very quick to respond to any questions/concerns we had. Everyone always took time to explain things in a way that we could understand. Even after moving away from Hawaii, everyone was very accommodating in working with time difference, and keeping an open line of communication.
The extensive research done to gather background information on our particular case also helped me learn more about everything that had gone on. That alone helped me feel more at ease with questions doctors couldn’t give me answers to.
We are very happy with the outcome of our case, and couldn’t have done it without them.
A heartfelt Mahalo to John, Geoff, Tricia, Rick, Tom and the rest of the team at Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina & Fairbanks! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Coins really nicez

In 2004, I was involved in a major motorcycle accident.  While I was in the trauma unit at Queen's on Oahu.  I was introduced to Attorneys Richard Fried, Gregory Lue-Kwan and later to Bert Sakuda. My first meeting they discussed How they would make sure I was treated fairly, both mentally and physically throughout the litigation/court proceedings.

Throughout these long proceedings they were there making sure all issues where being presented and covered. I felt, like I was there only client.  I highly recommend that if you are in need of legal help. There are none better the law firm of Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina & Fairbanks.  Thank you, William Fortini Jr.
reviews.ownerResponse:
William,
Thank you so much for sharing your experience and for the high recommendation. We truly appreciated your patience throughout the lengthy claim process. While it took some time to process the multiple stages of your recovery, we are glad that you were as pleased with the result as we were. Best of luck to you. Rick, Bert, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: My family engaged Cronin Fried based on the firm's exceptional reputation in medical malpractice litigation to represent us in a very unfortunate matter. We were not let down. We were especially impressed by the skill, diligence and sensitivity of our attorneys, John Thomas and Geoff Komeya, and their in-house medical investigator, Tom Duddy. Although no compensation will ever make up for the loss we suffered, their efforts produced an outcome that exceeded our expectations and also brought us an important measure of closure and vindication.
reviews.ownerResponse: We were grateful for the opportunity to represent your family and we hope that the resolution of the case has brought all of you some peace.  John, Geoff, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text:
A few years back i was the victim of a careless driver running a red light. My car was totaled and i sustained major injuries that required surgery and a lengthy rehabilitation. My insurance company  gave me a take it or leave offer of half of my car's value despite having submitted documentation to the contrary. I needed transportation so i had to accept their offer.
Going forward, i was fortunate that my long time beach volleyball
friend Wayne Kekina was a partner at the Cronin, Fried firm. My first call from the hospital was to him. Wayne has since retired but he turned my case over to Bert Sakuda. From the first day i walked into the office on crutches to the last when i walked out  with a check in my hand, i was made to feel like i was a part of their family by everyone i came in contact with. Over the period it took to settle my case i was amazed at the time and effort that was expended on my behalf. With some patience and the confidence that i was in great hands, i sat back as Bert went toe to toe with the insurance company. He was able to secure more than 5 times the amount of their initial low ball offer. Needless to say, i would highly recommend the Cronin, Fried firm to anyone that might require their expertise.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Jimmy,
We appreciate the review and recommendation. It really means a lot that our clients are happy with our service and would recommend our office to others in need of help. Our staff is enjoying your thoughtful gift of food and refreshment for the office. Thank you very much! We wish you the best in all of your future endeavors. The Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: I am beyond pleased and grateful for the superb professional service and excellent result I received after entrusting my personal injury matter to Bert Sakuda and his team.  His legal expertise, knowledge and skills in evaluating liability and damages as well as his skillful negotiating ability with insurance carriers make him the best in the legal profession.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Lynne,
We appreciate the trust you placed in our office. Our goal is always to do our very best and we are happy to hear that you were pleased with the result. It means a lot to receive such positive feedback. Bert and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text:
Words can't express our gratitude for the level of professional (and personal) care we received from our attorney Bert Sakuda and his vastly knowledgeable legal assistant Tricia Etsler to legally advocate for my case of being a pedestrian hit in a crosswalk. Highly recommended by a former judge, Bert put our concerns increasingly at ease from the first meeting by his calm demeanor and extensive knowledge about my kind of trauma. After that first meeting, my wife simply said, "He knows what he's talking about" as she had years of managing cases for returning combat soldiers wounded in the Middle East.
Throughout the duration of the case, they were a source of reassurance  with frequent communication in conference, emails, or phone calls. We felt encouraged whenever we talked on the phone.

From the start, my wife and I often felt the genuine care and empathy of the law firm went further than mere professionalism. Referring to previous clients' experiences, Bert helped me understand and accept my amnesia regarding how the accident happened, that I might never be able to make sense of the accident.

Simply put, our experience has been thankfully great-NOT that we'd wish for trauma on anyone to have that kind of legal care.

Mahalo ke Akua for Bert and Tricia.

Jimmy C
reviews.ownerResponse:
Jimmy,
It was our pleasure to be able to guide you through the process. We are humbled that you placed your trust in us. We know you will continue to heal and wish you the very best.
Bert, Tricia, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text:
Following a serious accident  while vacationing in Kauai, my family and I engaged the law firm of Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina, and Fairbanks.  What a great choice!
Because of their extensive experience with personal injury settlements, they were able to guide our family through difficult times, carefully explaining each step.  We knew we were in good hands.  I highly recommend them.
Thank you Rick, Bert, and Tricia.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Paula,
You are a remarkable person. Your strength and resiliency allowed you to persevere when the odds were stacked against you. We know that you will continue to heal and thrive. It was truly an honor to guide you through the legal process and we wish you the very best. Rick, Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: Our family's experience with Bert, Tricia, Rick and the entire firm was unparalleled. When our family member required representation, we were fortunate enough to receive them as a recommendation.  Over the course of our time working with this law firm, we experienced consistent and clear communication, as they took time to explain the legal process and where we stood.  Whenever we reached out to them with questions, responses were timely and thorough. Bert, Tricia and Rick made a point to build a relationship with our family, getting to know not only each person, but the distinct personalities involved. We were ushered through a difficult and complex time with confidence.  Without hesitation, my family and I recommend Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina & Fairbanks.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are deeply grateful for the heartfelt review.  It’s truly rewarding to know that we were able to provide the support and guidance that your family needed.  Our team is committed to helping clients navigate complex legal matters with compassion and expertise.  We’re honored to have been a part of your journey.  We wish you all the best.  Rick, Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: Our family was very fortunate to have worked with Bert, Tricia, Rick and staff to successfully come to a resolution for a family member who was seriously injured. These people are not only outstanding professionals, but they are good people who showed empathy and understanding toward our family as our loved one went through very challenging physical and psychological trauma. We recommend, without hesitation, this firm to help guide you and your family through the legal process toward a successful resolution.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with our firm.  We’re honored to have been able to assist your family during such a challenging time.  It’s incredibly rewarding to know that our efforts made a positive impact.  Best wishes to you and your family.  Rick, Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text:
I recently had the privilege and pleasure of working with Bert and Tricia at Cronin Fried to resolve my personal injury case.  They were very professional, answered all my questions and kept me informed throughout the entire process.  I was very pleased with the outcome and would highly recommend Cronin Fried to anyone who needs legal representation.

Thank you!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Craig,
We are so grateful for your positive feedback. We know legal matters can be stressful, so we are thrilled to hear that you were happy with our service and the result. Best of luck to you in the future. Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: I am so happy I chose this team to represent me. It shows they care about their clients. They are the best!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Christina,
Thank you so much for taking the time to leave a review. We appreciate your kind words and do very much care about our clients. We were happy to help and wish you the best of luck in the future. Bert, Tricia, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: We were tremendously blessed to have Bert and Tricia assist us with our sons motorcycle accident. Unfortunately he had a car hit him while they were crossing three lanes of traffic. My son was ejected from his bike and thrown into a metal median fence pole. His bike was in shambles and he had a long road to recovery and continues to recover. We hired Bert and his team. He understands the military, motorcycle accidents, etc. Their communication is second to none. My son is in Hawaii however, we as his parents are in Arizona. They kept us always informed, engaged and provided appropriate direction. Highly professional and knowledgeable in their area of expertise! We feel tremendously blessed for the representation we received. I would highly recommend Bert, Tricia and their team for any legal needs.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Melissa,
We very much appreciate the review. We take pride in our work and it is very rewarding to hear that you were happy with our service. You have a very special family and we wish you all the best. Bert, Tricia, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: The team at Cronin, Fried, Sekiya and Fairbanks was outstanding. They provided exceptional support to me and my family during a challenging and overwhelming legal process. From the outset, they were readily available to answer my questions and consistently followed up with expedience. Throughout every stage, I felt confident that they had my best interests at heart. Their professionalism and dedication to my case truly made a difference.
reviews.ownerResponse:
John,
You are an inspiration to all of us here at Cronin Fried. It was our honor and privilege to be able to help you through the legal process. Thank you for your trust in our firm. We wish you and Laurel continued success. Rick and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: Bert Sakuda and Tricia Etsler were amazing. My family and I were provided with outstanding service that made the process of our case go very smoothly. They helped us answer all the questions and made every detail understandable. I highly recommend seeking legal help/advice from Bert Sakuda and the team at Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina & Fairbanks.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Drew,
It is always humbling to hear our clients express gratitude for our efforts. We were happy to help guide you through the process and grateful that we were able to obtain a great result. We wish you the best of luck in the future. Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: Bert and Tricia made this process very easy for me. They did all the heavy lifting and communicated with me every step of the way. Their communication was very clear and effective. They are experts at their craft! They made sure explain things to me and break down what was happening throughout the entire process since I have no experience in this field. Thank you Bert and Tricia for all your help and taking care of me. You both made this a smooth and painless process!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Megan,
We appreciate you taking the time to post about your experience. It was our pleasure to represent you and obtain a successful recovery. We wish you the best as you move forward in your life. Bert, Tricia, and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text:
I first meet Bert Sakuda and Tricia Etsler in December of 2015.
I live on the Big Island and was rear-end, when I was at a stop light.
At the time , my wife was battling cancer.

There concern for my wife and my physical condition was overwhelming. Bert and his team put the well-being of their clients first.They are very professional, thorough, caring individuals.
Their focus is on the client, working hard to get the best possible out come.
I highly recommend the law firm of Cronin, Fried, Sekiya, Kekina & Fairbanks.
Bert & Tricia  are amazing.
Ronald Ford
Kailua Kona Hi
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ron,

Thanks for taking the time to post such a wonderful review. We were very happy to help and we appreciate the trust you put in our firm during a very difficult time.

Take care,
Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team

reviews.text: My sister was terribly hurt in a pedestrian accident in 2019.  A very close and astute friend of hers who is an attorney recommended that we meet with Bert Sakuda.  It was to be a pivotal meeting that came at a time of great pain, trauma and fear for our sister’s life.  We simply did not know what we did not know.  Bert Sakuda shepherded us through layers and layers of medical, legal and financial issues that seemed never ending.  Tricia Etsler was our  insurance and expenses expert who kept detailed records from the many different providers.  Bert was always well-informed of our situation and options open to us.  He and Tricia and the team were always accessible.  They brought a wealth of experience, resourcefulness and compassion that gave strength to endure through some very tough times and HOPE.  We give them the highest recommendation without reservation.  We are forever grateful for their professionalism, great negotiation skills and generosity of spirit.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Lisa,
Thank you for the very thoughtful, kind review. We were honored to be of service to your family and continue to be amazed by how your sister has persevered. It is truly a testament to the respect and love you have for one another. Thank you all for placing your trust in us. Aloha, Bert, Tricia and the Cronin Fried team.

reviews.text: He's AWESOME,
Great place for fur babies...staff super nice and loving to our pets 5 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Cozy with very loving and caring staff

Hired Leavitt Law Firm initially for what seemed to be an easy case to deal with a UCCJEA jurisdiction determination case involving my son. There were two custody cases going on in two different states and they were very responsive and informative throughout the whole process. Even after jurisdiction was transferred to Hawaii, they kept in touch with me and followed the Hawaii case ensuring everything would go smoothly in getting my son back. When the date came to get my son from Nevada, I felt at ease knowing they would be there in case anything went wrong. So thankful I had them there because nothing went as planned. The team at Leavitt Law moved EXTREMELY quick to do their best to do everything in their power to help me. They were able to file and certify a motion in Nevada within the same day and were with me every second guiding me to the next steps. I wouldn’t have known what to do without them. At the end of the day I had my son back in my custody. I highly recommend Leavitt Law Firm to anyone looking help regarding custody issues.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Review for Mark Bridges

I am proud to leave this review in recognition of the outstanding work of Mark A. Bridges, and the team at Ganz & Bridges Law Office LLLC. Facing an Administrative Separation Board was one of the most difficult experiences of my military career. The possibility of losing not only my career but also my benefits, my reputation, and my family’s stability was overwhelming. Retaining Mr. Bridges was the best decision my family and I have ever made, and his expertise ultimately secured the most important outcome I could have hoped for a unanimous 3 to 0 ADSEP Board vote for retention.

From the very first consultation, Mr. Bridges impressed me with his professionalism, honesty, and deep understanding of military law. He listened carefully to the details of my case, asked the right questions, and explained the process of the separation board in clear terms. Unlike others who might give false reassurance, he was realistic but he laid out the risks while also providing confidence that he would build the strongest possible defense on my behalf.

His preparation was second to none. He meticulously reviewed my service record, gathered evidence, and identified key character witnesses. More importantly, he took the time to understand me as a person, not just a client. That personal investment showed in the way he crafted my defense he highlighted not only my achievements and character but also my dedication to continued service.

Communication throughout the process was exceptional. Anytime I had questions or concerns, Mr. Bridges was quick to respond with patience and clarity. His compassion and reassurance gave me strength during moments of doubt. I never felt alone in the process he was always by my side every step of the way.

When the day of the board finally arrived, Mr. Bridges demonstrated exactly why he has earned such a strong reputation. He was composed, persuasive, and thorough. His ability to cross-examine character witnesses, present compelling arguments, and frame my service history in the best possible light was nothing short of masterful. It was clear that he had anticipated every angle of the case, and his advocacy left no doubt that I had the best representation possible.

Thanks to Mr. Bridges’s exceptional work, the board ruled in my favor, with a 3 to 0 vote to be retained. That outcome preserved my career, my benefits, and the future I had worked so hard to build for my family. I do not doubt that without his guidance and representation, the result could have been very different.

I wholeheartedly recommend Mark A. Bridges and Ganz & Bridges Law Office LLLC to any service member facing an Administrative Separation Board or any other military legal matter. His knowledge, preparation, and advocacy are unmatched, but just as important, he cares deeply about the people he represents. Five stars hardly seem enough he deserves ten stars.

Helped me out of the worst point in my life, I was being investigated for something I had no part in and was given the option to plea guilty to a lesser charge, the legal services offered to me on base recommended I just plea guilty and go my way. I instead sought legal counsel from Ganz & Bridges Law Office, LLLC having all criminal charges dropped. Truly provided me with the utmost professional and helpful legal assistance I could’ve asked for, my life would be extremely different without their help. Thank you again for everything you did.

I’ve already referred others to Mark and will continue to do so. If you need legal help and want someone who knows the law and takes the time to understand your case, I highly recommend Mark Bridges and the team at Ganz & Bridges Law Office LLLC.

reviews.text: MS. GANZ IS A PITBULL IN A SKIRT!!!!!!! THIS WOMAN SAVED MY LIFE!!!!! This is a long story. I was in the Army for 11 years and 8 months. Upon my duty to Hawaii I was assigned to my command. I went through a very nasty divorce and my command was not supportive. I was forced to move to a barracks room wherr junior soldiers lived as an nco (so let the targeting and shaming begin) after being homeless for a week sleeping on the beach and in my neighbors garage because my ex wife asked for a no contact order. She went to my command accusing me of sexual assault. My commander believed every word of it and almost surrendered my medical records to her without my consent. I became very depressed and turned to alcohol and other bad decisions. No one believed my story and rumors about me spread throughput the command because of the S1 shop. My wife made this report because I asked for the divorce. Two weeks later exact after I told my commander she was going to do this because she told me she was going to ruin my career. Last time we were intimate was january and she didn't report it till may.  My ex wife went as far as getting her mother to cooperate as a witness to the lie.  I would have had to commit this crime while my two children and her mother who lived with us would have been present.  My commander put me in pretrial confinement and they released me after 2 days cause he had no grounds. My commander then made it impossible for me i had to sign it at the CQ desk.  From 0630 till 2300 every 2 hours. Well he put me back in pre-trial confirment for 6 months. Well after months of research and discovering evidence Ms. Ganz was able to have the witness speak at the deposition where she was able to cross examine and pick her apart. She absolutely poked so many wholes in her story that they couldn't charge me with sexual assault. My commander was pushing for 45 years and i was released the next morning. Ms. Ganz got it down to time served. While I did plead guilty to the mistakes I made I dont have to bear that horrible burden of being seen as a rapist or a sex offender. This woman is a life saver and no amount of money can compare to how she will help you. If you are a member of the armed forces I highly highly recommend you use her for your services. You will not be disappointed.

reviews.text: I hired Ms. Ganz a few months back after being told I should sign a pretrial agreement that waived all my administrative separation rights so that the marine corps could separate me with an other than honorable discharge without having to do an admin sep board first. I actually wasn’t told that that’s what they were doing until I talked to Ms. Ganz. Another civilian military lawyer I talked to tried to scare me into hiring him and I’m glad I didn’t hire him because nothing he said would happen did happen. On my first consultation with Ms. Ganz she identified some mistakes that she thought the unit might make while trying to separate me and said to be on the lookout for them. She said we could use those things as leverage to get a better characterization of service because I wanted to get out anyway and I was actually on my way to EAS. I didn’t believe her at first, but then everything she said would happen happened. So then I started trusting her fully. She went up against a team of all male high ranking marine officers and military JAG lawyers to fight for me even though they tried over and over to beat her down, she didn’t let up, and we ultimately won. There were a bunch of others who were in the same position as me who didn’t hire her and told me they got kicked out with other than honorable discharges. I’ve been telling people that she is a bad ass lawyer and am writing this review so that other people in the military who are facing stressful legal trouble like mine will know what theyre getting.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Don't let anyone bully you and certainly not when doing so will negatively affect the rest of your life. An Other Than Honorable (OTH) has severe consequences and in my opinion it's totally unfair how the marine corps tries to hand them out like candy. I am stoked we got you that Honorable characterization of service. Thank you so much for taking the time to explain this to other service members who may be in similar situations. All the best going forward.
- NG

I want to warn all women, especially victims of violence, about my experience with this lawyer. Hiring him was the worst mistake of my life and a total disaster. From my experience, he does not understand abuse against women, and his professional ethics are seriously questionable.
Here’s why I strongly advise against trusting him:
Negligence and lack of respect:
While my life was on the line in court, he was busy texting about the colors of the furniture in his home. He didn’t prepare my defense, did no investigation because he thought it was “too much work,” and didn’t even know the basic details of my case.
Unethical financial conduct:
He took money from me that was not justified and was completely unethical. He demanded payment upfront and later tried to charge extra fees for the length of the trial, as if the delays were my fault. From my experience, his focus was on getting paid, not actually working on the case.
Severe professional incompetence:
He became overwhelmed when he received detailed information and clearly wasn’t capable of handling the case. Instead of defending me, he stayed silent in front of the judge, apparently afraid of saying the wrong thing or looking bad. His priority seemed to be protecting his own image in court, not defending me.
Lack of humanity and moral responsibility:
He ignored calls and didn’t support me when I needed him most. His public profile glorifies cases involving violent people he has defended, which to me shows a complete lack of moral responsibility. His words felt empty and fake, disguised as professionalism just to get his check.
Because of his absent and inadequate defense, my abuser — a man who physically assaulted me, threatened my life, and made me fear for my safety — is still free. Based on my experience, this lawyer is far from someone a victim should trust. Don’t waste your money or put your life in his hands.

Let me tell you that it would be a pleasure to provide the details of your lazy work, but I will leave it here so people can see the ferocity of your response without you even knowing which of your clients you are referring to.
What can be expected from a so-called lawyer with no real training, extremely green and inexperienced, lacking ethics, a liar, and who has just made it clear to everyone that—according to you—you know who I am and that I tried to harm an abuser?
You see, Alan, where are your principles as a lawyer you claim to be, if you admit that an alleged victim would want to harm an abuser? Nice try. Pathetic.
reviews.ownerResponse:
This is not a review from an actual client. It is likely from an opposing individual who lost their case to my client. The last paragraph of the review says that "a man who physically assaulted me, threatened my life, and made me fear for my safety - is still free."

The reviewer goes on to write, "[b]ased on my experience, this lawyer is far from someone a victim should trust."

These comments makes it clear that I represented the man who "is still free," not the woman who left the review. I am a defense attorney. I do not represent "victims." Victims are witnesses for the prosecution, not for defendants or defense attorneys.

I suspect I know who left this review. However, for privacy considerations I will not mention the reviewer's name. If my suspicions are correct, the judge found the reviewer to not be credible. She lied under oath and was evasive about answering straightforward questions. She lied about being abused for ulterior motives.

None of the other allegations make sense.

First, texting is not permitted in trial and the "color of furniture" does not interest me in the slightest.

Second, every client signs a written retainer agreement per the Hawaii Rules of Professional Conduct. "Extra fees" outside of the retainer agreement are not permitted.

Third, I do not ignore calls.

Lastly, I keep detailed records of all my clients, their cases, and the outcomes of their cases. This reviewer does not fit into any client profile that I have represented in at least the past 15 years (or more).

If this reviewer truly believes their allegations are accurate, I strongly encourage them to file a formal complaint, so I can refute each and every allegation instead of leaving anonymous reviews with no basis in fact.

Thank you.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Sabrina, for your kind words. It has been our pleasure working with you towards a prompt resolution of your injury claims. Your situation is a great reminder of the importance of having Uninsured (UM) and Underinsured (UIM) insurance on every vehicle in your household. With so many drivers in Hawai’i operating their vehicles on our roads without insurance (UM), or with insufficient insurance (UIM), to fairly or fully compensate people like you and your family when injured because of their inattentive or reckless driving, it is extremely important to protect yourself by purchasing UM and UIM insurance. And if you have more than one vehicle in the household, always elect the "stacking" option,  which allows you to combine all the policies if your injuries are serious enough. We wish you well and thank you for allowing us the opportunity to represent you. Aloha,  Dan, Suzanne, and Jocelyne

I can not stress how big of a help he has been to my family. I appreciate his help and advice.

If you are wondering how to navigate your own legal issues I would give Mr. Chen a call. He is one of the few people I really trust.

Mr. Daniel M. Chen was the opposing counsel in my case against a California company, and he NEVER returned my calls!  Instead, an associate attorney from the firm was assigned to litigate for their client.  I just WON the case as a Pro Se litigant (no attorney) on March 5, 2021 and I still haven't received any updates about my check or release papers I need to sign.  Mr. Daniel M. Chen is a partner at RUSH MOORE law firm, which is the same address he is using in his newly created private practice.

Mr. Daniel M. Chen was very unprofessional, engaged in unethical illegal practices, and never displayed any respect for me as a Pro Se litigant, which is required and mandated by the Hawaii State Bar Association.  A report was filed with the Council for Disciplinary Action that was accepted, and an ongoing investigation is taking place for all the Hawaii Rules of Conduct violations that he was involved in either directly or indirectly.

I would not trust an attorney who refused to take five minutes out of his day to return a call to an opposing party, Pro Se litigant, especially when the rules clearly indicate very concisely that special consideration and respect is a mandatory requirement in the Hawaii Rules of Conduct.

Please do not trust him!  He will be sanctioned soon by the Council for Disciplinary Action for violating these Rules:
Rule 4.3 (a) Dealing with Unrepresented Person
5.1 (a)(b) (c~1,2) ~ Responsibilities of Partners, Managers, and Supervisory Lawyers
8.4 (a)(c) ~ Misconduct

Thank you for reading my review.
You have been warned not to trust Mr. Daniel M. Chen!

2026 update: I am feeling misrepresented by my attorney and the paralegal Jennifer's written email to me. They are stating that I am not settling and taking action to withdraw as my lawyer instead of answering my questions about what I am signing. After spending thousands of dollars, I am quite disappointed.

Pressuring someone to sign something they do not understand should never be the norm, and certainly not championed by a lawyer who is supposed to be an advocator of justice.

** DO NOT SIGN ANYTHING UNLESS YOU UNDERSTAND WHAT YOU ARE SIGNING.

—-

Mr. Sink and Jennifer Inouye have absolutely help me come out on top after a hit and run.  I can't thank them enough for all of their hard work.

Tl;dr I can’t afford to hire a lawyer out-of-pocket, but he was super helpful anyway! We had property damage (but no injuries)  from a car accident due to negligent auto services, so I reached out to a legal referral service for possible contingency based service or even just legal advice. They gave me the names of two law offices. The first basically said “We don’t do that. Good luck. And if you want to hire us it’s $350/hr and a $10,000 retainer”. But Mr. Sink called me back himself to see if anyone had responded to my email, and then gave me some starting advice for gathering evidence and writing a letter to the auto service company, before trying for small claims court. It seems small but hearing advice like that from a lawyer goes a long way when you don’t know where to start to recoup your losses. His return call however brief, was free legal advice. He could have ignored the inquiry as a non viable lead, as did the other firm. Instead, he took the time to set me in the right direction and that bit of good will says a lot imo.

I was referred to Mr.Sink’s office through HSBA, and just had a phone consultation regards to employment issue I was going through. They got back to me very fast, and everything was really straightforward. I gained lots of clarity and support from him. I decided not to pursue the lawsuit because mine wasn’t going to make a strong case after all. Now I know who to contact if our family need legal advices. Thank you for your service!

Both times we got the outcome I needed.

So if you find yourself needing an attorney, he is the one to call and if he can't take your case he will refer you to another attorney who can help.

Thank you Willie!!!

Bill Lawson is Highly Recommended! Following is our story about our 6-year struggle to obtain Justice for our daughter. Mr Lawson was there with us step by step all the way to a very successful completion. He was more than just our attorney -- he was somebody we could could speak with about our struggles and he would reach out and help.

Mr. Lawson was retained in late 2014 on behalf of our daughter after she sustained a Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) as the result of an automobile accident while on vacation in Hawaii. We live in Oregon. This case involved three different insurance companies and several individuals. It was a complicated case because the injury (TBI) cannot be observed externally. This lawsuit dragged on from 2014 to July 2020 because each insurance company had to be convinced to settle.

Mr. Lawson kept us appraised every step of the way. At certain points in the years long process it was discouraging. But with Mr. Lawson’s patience and perseverance a successful final settlement was reached. Mr. Lawson was methodical in his planning and approach to help us obtain the best outcome. He engaged critical subject matter experts that really helped uncover all the facts and magnify the negligence in our daughter’s case. Even with the covid-19 problem in 2020, he managed to move the case forward. He never gave up and the outcome of the case was better than expected.

One great thing about Mr. Lawson is he genuinely cares. Through the years he was instrumental in recommending areas of treatment that we should investigate for our daughter. Not living in Hawaii made the lawsuit more difficult but Mr. Lawson’s excellent communications via text messaging, e-mails and phone conversations helped us through the entire process. We will always be grateful that Mr. Lawson was on our side.

Thank you Mr Lawson for your encouragement and support for six years. Well done!

I am a former client of Bill Lawson.  He is trustworthy, a man of integrity and very tenacious!

I appreciate that Mr. Lawson is so accessible!  You never feel ignored or neglected when he is helping you with a case.  If you have a question or concern, he will address it.

reviews.text:
No No No. Just got off of the phone with Johnathan, I believe he is the lead attorney. Actually, he hung up in my face and REFUSED TO CANCEL MY MEMBERSHIP!!!

A year ago I paid several fees to have access to a lawyer for a medical dispute. After weeks of waiting for someone to contact me, I had to call to receive help. A few days later, a man called me. I asked him if he was a lawyer, and he told me no. They told me that I wouldn't get a lawyer unless my case went to trial. So this man, who is not an attorney, had me give him detailed information, all of which was given in my enrollment. It was obvious that he hadn't read my profile and had just be told to call me.

Needless to say, the man was of little help, and didn't give me anymore information than the hospital I was in the dispute with gave me. They actually helped me MORE!

So a year later (today) I decided to cancel my membership because my family needs every penny we can save. I am paying $25 a month. I called Johnathan, he answered and pretended that he couldn't hear me. So, I emailed him, asking to cancel my membership. That's simple enough, right? NO!

Johnathan called me back 20 minutes after I sent the email, talking and talking about how busy he is. I asked him to cancel my membership. He and I went back and forth. He attempted to sale me a lower rate. I politely declined. He tried to scare me into thinking I would need them, I politely declined. He then became upset, began laughing, and telling me that I have all of these perks and he will give me 3 months free. Meanwhile he keeps double talking, telling me that I can cancel, but refusing to actually cancel.

I politely declined for the 5th time, the Johnathan said "Have a good day lady (while laughing)" and hung up the phone. This is an agency that I have been giving my hard earned money to. They  DID NOT EVER help me with anything. I didn't want anything but to politely cancel.

So as of today, I'm going to pull some strings and check into this racket. I'll return to these reviews with an update after some investigation. I understand that lawyers aren't expected to be the most polite people, however, this was flat out disrespectful, and not an acceptable way to speak to a client, especially one who is struggling to give you $25 a month for NOTHING.

These high reviews can not be real. I would check into these profiles before I gave any of these "reviews" any merit. Unless they intentionally discriminated against me, I take my experience to be a common practice amongst Attorney's For Freedom Law Firm.

FIND ANOTHER AGENCY! DO NOT GO HERE! THEY ARE NOT DIGNIFIED

I'm off to the BBB.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I sincerely apologize for the confusion regarding your request to cancel. We have reached out to you twice this week to discuss this matter, but we have received no response. In all cases, we endeavor to offer the highest level of service and professionalism.

Jonathan is not an attorney at our firm. He is our Director of the Attorneys on Retainer Program. According to our email records, Jonathan did cancel your membership within 20 minutes of your original email request. I'm not sure why this was not properly communicated to you. Jonathan often follows up with people in an effort to deliver the highest possible customer service.

Please feel free to contact me personally at marc@attorneysforfreedom.com so we can discuss this matter. I'm committed to treating you as I would want to be treated myself. As you can see by our many 5-Star reviews, we endeavor to treat everyone with professionalism, respect and kindness in all cases.

Marc J. Victor

After over a week of making calls, sending emails and being shut down it felt like I had 30 seconds left in the last round of a championship fight.
Gassed out, eyes almost shut, backed into a corner by a 16-0 opponent trying to keep the fight from going to the judges.

If you know the feeling, you can understand how time slows down, the room goes silent and you hear one voice yelling from your corner "Weave hook!" ...and in that moment of clarity you dig deep and blindly, weave, hook and connect. Not for the win, but just enough to finish the round still standing.

That feeling of losing a match, but winning hope is hard to explain.

Its a lesson you can only learn by staying in the fight and not giving up, knowing that when it comes right down to it, and you need help, you have someone in your corner to help you learn from your mistakes, train, come back and fight another day.

That is what it feels like working with Michael Healy.

He sees openings, strategies and angles no one else sees, and he keeps you in the fight even when you almost like feel its over.

Easily one of the best attorneys to work with in Hawaii bar none.

He was way more approachable and made me feel comfortable from that first call. He also charged a flat rate, because it seemed like it was going to be straightforward and simple and I felt that it was a fair price. Now after representing my daughter against an atrocious opposing counsel, I'm sure he will carefully screen his next TRO client...the opposing counsel was very weird and would file motions and carry on with excessive emails back and forth, then the court stuck to what was stated in the first place. I'm sure if he were to bill me per hour, we would have exceeded the flat rate amount. Mitchell was very kind to my daughter, explained things to her in ways she could understand, and represented her very well. I hope you never need to hire a lawyer, but if you find yourself needing one, I highly recommend him!

I've recently had the good fortune of being represented by Attorney Dean Hoe and felt compelled to share my experience, a testament to his exceptional skills and dedication. Legal situations can be overwhelming, but having someone as proficient and professional as Dean on your side alleviates a significant amount of stress.

From the moment I initially reached out, Dean was prompt, thorough, and attentive. He immediately began crafting a strategic approach to my case, diving into the details, and making sure every aspect was covered meticulously. In just a few weeks, he managed to secure a dismissal for my case.

What truly sets Dean apart is his extraordinary ability to make complex legal matters simple to understand. He walked me through every step of the process, ensuring I knew what was happening at all times. This made me feel supported and at ease despite the circumstances. His authentic concern for my situation was both comforting and encouraging.

Furthermore, Dean's communication skills are unmatched. I was kept informed and updated at all times, never left in the dark. He was always readily available to answer any questions or concerns I had, never making me feel like an afterthought.

Dean Hoe is not just an attorney, but an advocate who fights fiercely for his clients. He displayed nothing but the utmost respect, integrity, and professionalism throughout the entirety of my case. The results speak for themselves - a swift and successful resolution that far exceeded my expectations.

In a world where it's often difficult to find a reliable and trustworthy attorney, Dean Hoe is a shining beacon. If you find yourself in need of legal assistance, I cannot recommend him highly enough. His representation was, without a doubt, the best decision I made during my legal ordeal. Dean, thank you for your unparalleled service and for giving me peace of mind throughout this process.

Highly recommended!

reviews.text: Bill is certainly in the top tier of Hawaii's defense lawyers, and his fees are quite reasonable compared with those of his more arrogant and media-hungry colleagues. I was facing a pretty serious federal charge, and he negotiated a plea agreement that resulted in a very light sentence. The prosecutor talked to me right after sentencing, and told me Bill had "saved my life." I can't think of a better recommendation than that. As others have mentioned, he won't return all your calls, or reply to all your emails. There's a lot of anxiety associated with being on the wrong end of a criminal case, so I considered knocking a star off for that. But you just have to trust him. The impression I got is that he's focused on whatever case is most urgent at the time. But when it's your turn, you have his complete attention, and he really knows what he's doing. Bill's a friendly guy, highly respected by his colleagues, and driven to get the best possible results for his clients. I highly recommend him.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mahalo for taking time to review us. We take pleasure in helping others navigate the court system!!!

1) I paid 1k for an early termination petition and expungement that was guaranteed, I just needed an attorney to get me in front of the judge.  Initial conversations were brief but bakke seemed willing to take the job and see it through.
They had me use their company portal to communicate with them .  Over time I would check in with them to see if a court had been set up yet and they would respond in a reasonable amount of time.
When I finally went to court bakke seemed kinda rushed like he was juggling a lot of cases as he had me and several other clients present at the same time. Wasn’t really an issue. However when the case was concluded and I was granted early termination I stepped outside the court room where Bakke explained that his paralegal will handle the rest and we will be in touch.
They than proceeded to send me court paperwork with another clients name on it.  When I asked why would they send me paperwork with someone else’s name on it the responses would come back to me very slow. It took about 2 months for them to get me paperwork with my name on it, however the paperwork was incomplete .  I messaged them through their portal and called them on several occasions to help rectify the situation to which I have received no response.
Overall I’d give him 2 stars for his mannerism, but it seems as if once he gets the money he moves on and forgets about you even if the job is incomplete. Im still currently trying to rectify the situation and would add a star or 2 if we can get the job done but I currently cannot give stars or rating for an incomplete job.
Update:  I got ahold of Mr Bakke and he is definitely a busy man.  He was honest with me and took responsibility for the mishap. He and his team have taken the necessary steps to get this chapter closed in my life to which I’m really grateful for. I would give 4.5 stars if I could. This is Robert Gentry, President of the Gold Coast Neighborhood Association in Honolulu, Hawaii.  We are a 501(c)(3) neighborhood organization comprised of 10 ocean front condo and co-op buildings with about 500 units.  We were founded in 2002.  The indigenous Hawaiians built a sea wall along the ocean (in front of what became our properties) almost 100 years ago, way before our current community existed.  This sea wall provided ocean access to the shoreline, and to the ocean throughout the entire time of it's existence.  Shortly after the formation of our neighborhood group we petitioned the State of Hawaii to repair the 100 year old structure and make it safe for the public to access the oceanfront and the ocean.  The State never took the responsibility to repair the structure, so our elected State representatives in the House and the Senate urged us to file a law suit.  We did just that and engaged Robert Klein to handle our case.

His professional experience and his expertise was exemplary.  His knowledge of the law, the State, and the issues brought us a successful ruling from both the Circuit Court and the 3 member Appeals Court.  Both said that the State is responsible for the sea wall, and in fact owns the facility, and all the land under the sea wall.    The State appealed the case to the Hawaii Supreme Court, and it ruled that the State indeed owns an easement across the top of the sea wall for public access to the ocean front, and the ocean.  Now we have a majority of jurists, looking at this case, ruling in behalf of our issue for public access and safety.  And, they have firmly assigned the liability of any injury to the State of Hawaii.  Robert Klein's ability in this complicated case was extraordinary,  for he was, and is, committed to ocean access on behalf of the public.  He now has helped establish firm case law for the future of safe ocean access for the people of Hawaii.  It will be very difficult for future developers and private property owners to prohibit the public from their right to the ocean in Hawaii  Our organization speaks very highly of Robert Klein, and urges potential clients to consider engaging him, and his team,  in any legal action they may need.  Your action will not be regretted!!  Aloha!

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Will, for your review and continued trust in us. It's been pleasure working with you for your many real estate needs.

reviews.text: We were unfortunately recommended by Chuck Choi to use his service for Tenant by Entirely.  For this service he charged us double the amount that it should take.  Typically is about $450.  He charged us $950.  In addition he screwed up the paperwork thus delaying the TE by a month and wasted our time with another office visit.  Would not recommend him for anything.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Ebaugh, this is the first negative review I have received in my 13 years of practicing law in Hawaii and it's important that I take a moment to respond. First, we appreciate your feedback and understand your frustration. As you noted, you requested a deed to hold property as Tenants by the Entirety with your wife. However, because your property is registered in land court and your wife changed her name for the marriage, this required an additional Land Court Petition be filed with the Land Court noting that name change. This is a separate document filed with the Land Court and recorded with the Bureau of Conveyances once approved. Our fees are in line with current market rates and are actually more reasonable than most because our flat rates include all filing fees, recording fees, and all taxes. I'm sorry you were disappointed with the charges, but all of this was explained to you prior to the commencement of any legal work or incurring any charges. I'm also sorry this process took longer than you or we would have liked, but I'm glad we were able to get your Land Court Petition and Deed completed and recorded. It's unfortunate you were disappointed with our services as we pride ourselves in offering the best possible services to all our clients. Thank you.
